# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-07-13

NebuSec says GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499) affects every major Linux distribution since 2011 and can be turned into a 97% stable privilege escalation and container escape.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. GhostLock, a stack-UAF that has existed in all Linux distributions for 15 years
   Decision-changing local privilege-escalation issue for anyone shipping Linux hosts or containers.

2. Show HN: Mindwalk – Replay coding-agent sessions on a 3D map of your codebase
   Useful if you review or debug coding-agent behavior and want a faster read on scope creep or verification gaps.

3. Protobuf-py: Protobuf for Python, without compromises
   Worth a look if Python protobuf ergonomics or generated code readability are a bottleneck in agent or backend tooling.

4. My 2 API products make $5K/month combined. What actually grew them
   Concrete acquisition tactics for developer APIs are more useful than generic growth advice, especially the LLM-surfaced competitor-page angle.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-07-13
